bullets.text: At least 12 people, including two children, were killed in Ukraine's capital following massive Russian drone and missile attacks on Kyiv flats.
Reports indicate that a vessel described as a 'floating armoury' in the Gulf of Oman has been seized by Iranian military personnel.

A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to return a Colombian woman, Adriana Maria Quiroz Zapata, who was deported to the Democratic Republic of Congo, calling her deportation 'likely illegal'.
New research suggests a link between seismic testing for oil and gas exploration and decreased commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.
Federal budget funding for Canberra roads, including $50 million to duplicate a road in West Belconnen, could potentially increase peak-hour traffic, according to transport planning experts.

Opposition leader Angus Taylor has responded to the federal budget, proposing a significant migration cut and $22.5 billion in tax cuts, including indexation of lower tax brackets to inflation starting in 2028-29.
The AI rally continues to fuel stock market momentum, with the S&P 500 topping 7,500 and US stocks reaching new records after upbeat earnings from Cisco Systems Inc. and a landmark AI IPO.
Active managers are struggling to keep up with the market, with only 1 in 4 beating the market as the AI rally is driven by a small group of tech megacaps.

The proliferation of AI-generated and AI-assisted text on the internet raises concerns about a potential degradation in semantic and stylistic diversity, factual accuracy, and other negative developments.
